title: cyclophyllum urophyllum
intro: Cyclophyllum urophyllum, also known as the 'Tall Ironwood', is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae, native to tropical regions of the Pacific Islands. It has been found to possess various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal activities, which are attributed to the presence of bioactive compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Cyclophyllum urophyllum include its potential to treat fever, respiratory infections, and gastrointestinal issues, as well as its ability to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing. The plant can be used in various herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is often used in traditional medicine practices in the Pacific Islands.
